ety: pać˚ = Prk. पच्च(इ) = S. पच्य(ते), pass. of rt. पच्
def: v.n. To undergo digestion, be concocted in the stomach, be digested; to be swallowed, eaten up, devoured, consumed, disposed of, done away with so as not to be disgorged (property, &c. wrongfully or dishonestly possessed of)
more: v. n. S. पच् to cook. 1. To be digested, assimilated. 1. Itnā khāo jitnā pache . Prov. Eat no more than you can digest. 2. Kutte ko ghī nahīṅ pachtā . Prov. Dogs cant digest butter (The shallow pate good fortune doth intoxicate). 2. To be swallowed, eaten up; devoured; spent (ill-gotten gain). Rupayā pach gayā . 3. To labour hard; grind; drudge; sweat. Pachtè pachte mar gayā . 4. To be tired out, exhausted, done up. Maiṅ pati to sūṅ kah pachī, tajo na man abhimān, Tirlokī meṅ na lakho jodhā Ap samān . Addressed to Rāvan by his wife Mandodrī. I've talked, my husband, till I'm tired—you wont give up your pride— No doughty foe like Ram there is in all the three worlds wide. 5. To be kept close, secret, to oneself; to keep one's own counsel; to be sealed (one's lips). Peṭ kī halkī hai, ek din na pachī rāt kī bāt . Wom Jān Sāhab. pach marnā , v. n. To be worked to death. pach hārnā , v. n. To…
v. n. 1. To be digested. 2. To rot. 3. To be consumed; to take pains, to labour.
subs: spent, or disposed of (property, &c.) as to permit no fear of detection and punishment; to be swallowed and enjoyed without repugnance or remorse: to be, or to lie, soaking in any mess; to rot, ferment, putrefy; to labour hard (at, -par), take pains, drudge, sweat, grind; to be tired out, exhausted; to be concealed, kept close (a secret)
v.n. To be worked to death
v.n. To toil and lose, to labour in vain.

more: n. f. An epithet of Lakshmī, the goddess of wealth, or chief goddess.

def: s.f. A lotus, Nelumbium speciosum; a multitude of lotuses, or a lake abounding in them; a particular magical art, witch-craft; a woman of the first and most excellent of the four classes into which the sex is distinguished: (The four classes are, Padminī, Ćītriṇī, Sankhinī, and Hastinī).
more: n. f. A woman of the first or most excellent of the four classes into which women are divided. The others in their order are Chatarnī, Hastinī , and Sankhinī . The last is a soubriquet for a very ugly woman. Khudā ne padmanī ko qaum meṅ inkī kīyā paidā, Baṛā har ek se rutbah na kyoṅ samjheṅ chamār apnā ! Jān Sāhab. Once in their tribe a handsome woman God did make, Shall not the cobblers then o'er other tribes rank take?
s. f. A description of woman, the most excellent of the four classes into which womankind is divided by the Hindūs. (The names of the other three are chitriṇī, sankhinī and hastinī ).

subs: (padma+āsan), s.m. A lotus-seat, seat or throne in the shape of a lotus (esp. one on which idols are placed); a particular posture in religious meditation (sitting with the thighs crossed with one hand resting on the left thigh, the other held up with the thumb upon the heart, and the eyes directed to the tip of the nose)
(padma+āk˚), s.m. Lotus-pool, a large deep tank or pond abounding in lotuses
(padma+āl˚), s.f. 'Whose dwelling is in the lotus,' an epithet of Lakshmī
s.m. The petal of a lotus; — Costus speciosus
s.m. Name of a purāṇa describing the period during which the world was a lotus
s.m. The plant Pterospermum acerifolium, or Webera corymbosa
s.f. The small tree Hibiscus mutabilis
s.m. Resin of the Pinus longifolia
s.m. Name of a fragrant wood used in medicine (and described as cooling and tonic)
s.m. 'The lotus-born,' an epithet of Brahma
s.f. A species of prickly nightshade, Alhagi maurorum
(padma+ut˚), s.m. The plant Carthamus tinctorius, safflower

def: adj. & s.m. Addicted to farting; — a farter; (met.) one easily frightened, a coward ( = pādū; padakkaṛ).
more: (from پاد or पर्द्द ) adj. Having the quality of farting. ( Met. ) A coward.

def: s.m. See s.v. pāda. s.m. Foot; leg; root of a tree; foot of a mountain; hill at the foot of a mountain; bottom of a bag (syn. pāṅw, pā, pāʼe); — ray, beam; — fourth part, quarter; (in Pros.) fourth part of a śloka, stanza, or verse; line of poetry

pādahat (S. pāda+āhat), part. adj. Struck with the foot, kicked; trodden; touched with the foot

s.f. = pahunaʼī, q.v. s.f. Hospitality, entertainment of guests or visitors.
s.f. Hospitality, entertainment, reception (of guests or visitors)
subs: (-kī), To practise hospitality, to entertain, act the host, to receive.

def: s. f. Hospitality, entertainment.
s.f. Arrival; reach; access, admittance; attainment, comprehension; sagacity, penetration; capacity, power; — receipt, acknowledgment.
s.m. The fore-arm, the wrist.

ety: caus. of pahuṅćnā; the ā˚ of pahuṅćā˚ = Prk. caus. aff. आवे or आव = S. आपि
def: v.t. To cause to arrive, cause to reach; to cause to be sent; to convey, escort, conduct, accompany, see (one) to (a place), to bring; to send, forward, transmit; to cause, occasion, bring to pass; to inflict (punishment, misery, &c.) upon
subs: v.t. To cause to arrive or to reach, to send, forward, transmit; to convey, conduct, &c.

def: v.t. (Braj) = pahǒṅćānā.
n. m. S. प्र + अञ्च to go. 1. Arrival. 2. Access ( rasāī ); admittance; ingress. 3. Reach; grasp; scope; comprehension. 4. Capacity; power; authority. 5. Receipt; acknowledgement ( rasīd ).
more: (from پـﮩنچنا) s. f. 1. Arrival, reach; sagacity, penetration. 2. Access, admittance. 3. A receipt.

def: s. m. The wrist.

v. a. 1. To cause to be sent; to remit; transmit; convey; conduct; escort; usher; transport; carry. Jhūṭe ko ghar tak pahoṅchā dīje . Prov. The liar should be escorted home. (His lie should be brought home to him). 2. To bring about; occasion; cause. anjām ko pahuṅchānā , v. a. 1. To carry out, or into effect; accomplish; fulfil. 2. To procure; supply; provide; purvey. zarar-i-shadīd pahuṅchānā , G. G. To cause grievous hurt to. nuqsān pahuṅchānā , v. a. To cause hurt; to injure.
more: (caus. of پـﮩنچنا) v. a. To cause to arrive, convey, transmit, bring, conduct, to cause, to occasion.

def: s. f. An ornament worn on the wrist, a kind of bracelet.

v. n. 1. To reach; get to; come up to or with; make; overtake; arrive; attain. 2. To feel; get; receive; suffer. 1. Sek pahuṅchnā . 2. Taklīf pahuṅchnā . 3. To have access to; to reach; grasp; to be equal to. baham pahuṅchnā , v. n. To reach; attain.
more: v. n. To arrive, reach, extend, to amount, to befall, belong.

def: (causal of پـﮩنچنا) v. a. To cause to arrive, to cause to convey or conduct.
s.f. An ornament worn on the wrist, a bracelet (esp. a bracelet of gold beads strung together and fastened with a clasp).

ety: pahuṅć˚ = Prk. पहुच्च(इ) = S. पर्याप्नो(ति); or fr. S. प्र+भू; or उप+सञ्जर्
def: v.n. To arrive, reach, come (upon), come (to or up to); to accrue, extend, befall; to have access (to).
ex: ہَوَائی جَہاز دُبَئی تَک پَہُن٘چا ہَے۔
havāī jahāz dubaī tak pahũcā hai.
The airplane has reached as far as Dubai.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀳𑀼𑀘𑁆𑀘 (pahucca) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: v.n. = pahuṅćnā, q.v. v.n. To arrive, reach, come (upon), come (to or up to); to accrue, extend, befall; to have access (to).
v.t. = pahǒṅćānā, q.v.
s.f. A female guest.
s.m. A flower = pushp, q.v.
v.n. To lie down, &c. = pauṛhnā, q.v. v.n. To lie down, repose, rest ( = paṛnā).
v. n. To lie down, to repose, to lie, to rest.
s.f. See پائي pāʼī.
s.f. A small copper coin; the fourth part of an ānā or anna, a paisā or pice; a vertical line (in Hindī accounts) denoting the fourth part of an anna; the twelith part of an anna, a pie.
s.f. Pins used by weavers to wind off the warp from; a light walking cane or stick, a switch
subs: v.t. To brush or clean the warp after it has been stretched for weaving.

ety: contrac. of pāʼe, or S. पाद्य
def: s.m. The foot; footstep; track, trace, mark, vestige; pursuit; — nerve, tendon, sinew; — adv. In the footsteps (of, -ke), in the track (of), in pursuit (of), following; after, behind ( = pīćhe); by reason (of), on account (of), therefore
more: s. m. 1. (Sans. पाद ) The foot. پي آنا pai ānā, To have a splint (a horse). پي کرنا pai karnā, To plague, to seize, to be importunate. 2. A nerve, tendon, sinew. 3. adv. Behind, after, following, therefore, in pursuit, by reason, on account. پي به پي pai-ba-pai, or پي درپي pai-dar-pai, adv. One after another, successively.
1. ( पयस् ) s. m. Milk, water. 2. ( उपरि ) adv. On, upon, over, at, to, nevertheless, yet. 3. conj. But.
subs: pai-ba-pai, pai-dar-pai, adv. Step by step; one after another, in succession, successively, consecutively; repeatedly, continuously, incessantly
v.n. To have a splint (a horse)
s.m. A privy; cesspool; — filth, dirt ( = pāʼe ḵẖāna, q.v.)
pai-rau (see rav or rau) adj. & s.m. Following; — follower

s.f. Following, going after; pursuit; prosecution; effort, exertion, endeavour; following the example (of), imitation; adherence; observance, compliance; hunting (after), search, quest, investigation; consequence; precedent

(-kī), To go after, follow, pursue, trace, track, seek, hunt (for); to prosecute, conduct (a suit, &c.); to continue, persevere (in a course); to follow the example (of), to imitate; to observe, comply (with)
(-kā), To follow, pursue; to be importunate; to persecute, &c. ( = pīćhā karnā, q.v.)
pai-kaṛhā, s.m. pai-kaṛī, s.f. Iron chains (for the legs of convicts), fetters;-ornamental rings worn on the ankles, anklets
(-par), To trace, track, get scent (of), obtain a clue (to); to find out, discover; to comprehend.

def: Picking cotton or safflower. Aurat paī ko gaī hai .
more: n. f. A box; a chest.

def: adv. See āge pīchhe (1) under آگے—Step by step; successively; consecutively ( lagā-tār ).
s.m. Non-resident cultivator; temporary occupant of village land; tenant at will
more: n. f. Pins used by weavers to wind off the warp from. pāī karnā , v. n. To brush or clean the warp after it has been stretched for weaving.
n. v. 1. One-fourth of an anna; a pice (Natives). 2. A small copper coin equal to one-twelfth of an annā; a pie. 3. A vertical line denoting one-fourth of an anna in Hindi accounts.
subs: s.f. Fallow land; — pāʼe-kār, s.m. = pāʼe-kāsht
pāʼe-kāsht, s.m. Non-resident cultivator; cultivation by non-residents.

def: s.m. Turn, twist, &c. = peć, q.v. s.m. Turn, winding; revolution; involution; convolution; — twist, coil; plait, fold; — entanglement, complication, maze, perplexity, intricacy, ambiguity; hitch, difficulty, trouble; trick (in wrestling, &c.); artifice, stratagem; wiliness, craftiness; duplicity, deceit; — a roll (of cloth, &c.); a screw; a machine
subs: s.m. & adj. Twist upon twist, all convolution; all intricacy, &c.; — involved, intricate, &c.
v.n. To experience trouble, suffer distress or loss
v.n. The twist (of anything) to become loose; a screw to come out
(-meṅ), A difficulty or hitch to arise (in); to occur as a difficulty or obstacle; to become complicated, difficult, or intricate; — to be entangled (in)
v.n. An artifice or stratagem to be successful; to prevail by stratagem, &c.
(-ke), To bind the twists (of a turban) with care
adj. Twisted, convoluted, coiled, spiral; winding, sinuous; intricate, complicated, involved; knotty, difficult; ambiguous; crooked, tortuous; artful, tricky, wily, crafty, deceitful
adj. Coil within coil; involved, entangled, intricate, complex
(-ko), To wind round, to twist; to screw; to circumvent, to deceive
(-meṅ), To entangle (the string of an adversary's paper kite); to throw obstacles or difficulties (in the way of)
(-meṅ; or -se), To practise artifices or tricks, to trick, deceive; to complicate; — to grapple (with, in wrestling)
v.n. To be twisted, &c.; to coil; to writhe; to be perplexed; to fall into difficulties, to sustain a loss
or khul-jānā, v.n. To be untwisted; to be unscrewed
(-se); To entangle (the silk of one's paper kite with that of another kite), to fight paper kites; — to meet, to embrace
peć-tāb, s.m. Twisting and twining; convolution, twisting knots, folds; contortions; restlessness, anxiety, agitation, perplexity, disquietude, distraction, distress; vexation, anger, indignation
v.n. To twist and turn; to writhe; to suffer distress or anxiety; to be vexed.

def: s.f. = H پيچهاننا पैछानना paićhānnā, v.t. See pahćān and pahćānnā.
v.t. = H پيچهان पैछान paićhān, s.f. See pahćān and pahćānnā.

ety: Zend paiti+dayā, rt. dī; S. prati+rt. dhī
def: part. adj. Born, created, generated, produced; invented, discovered, manifested, manifest, exhibited; procured, acquired, earned, gained; — s.f. That which is earned, earnings, gain, profit, income; interest; emoluments; perquisites, bribes (syn. yāft)
more: part. adj. 1. Born; created. Paidā huā, nā-paid ke wāste . Prov. What is born is to be destroyed again. 2. Produced; discovered; manifest; exhibited. 3. Procured; acquired; gained; amassed. paidā , n. f. 1. Gain; profit; earning; income; interest; drawings. 2. Emoluments; perquisites; bribes ( yāft ). paidā karnā , v. n. 1. To bring into being; create; breed; bring forth; hatch. 2. To produce; draw out; raise. Woh patthar meṅ se roṭī paidā kartā hai . He extracts bread from stone. (He is very hard-working—he'll never starve). 3. To originate; invent; evoke; give rise to; bring to pass. 4. To form; make; contract; enter into. 5. To come by; earn; obtain; realize; clear.

adj. Born, created, produced, exhibited, discovered, invented. پيداکرنا paidā karnā, v. a. To discover, produce, breed, find, to form, create, occasion, get, earn, gain, invent, procure. پيداوار paidā-wār, s. m. Produce of a field, fruit, profit of trade, &c. پيداهونا paidā honā, 1. To be born, to be created. 2. To be found, to be earned, to be produced.
subs: v.t. To bring into being, to create, make, form, produce, give rise to, occasion; to raise, bring forth, breed, hatch; to originate, invent, discover; to find; to procure, acquire, earn, gain, realize
v.n. To be born; to be created, &c.; to arise, spring up, be produced; to be found; to be earned, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پیدا (paidā). Compare Kashmiri پٲدٕ (pạ̄dụ).

def: s.f. Birth; creation, production; origin, rise; produce; earnings, profits, &c. (see paidā); — one of the titles of the Book of Genesis.
ex: یہ غریبی سرکاری بدعنوانی کی پیدائش ہیں.
This poverty is a genesis of governmental corruption.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پَیدَائِش (paydā'iš).

def: n. f. See ابتدا Birth; creation. Dunyā kī paidāish kā hāl koī nahīṅ jāntā . The world's beginning no man knoweth.
more: (from پيدا) s. f. Birth, creation, production; earnings, produce, profit.

def: adj. Natural; original; inborn, innate, &c. (syn. aṣlī; jibillī; ḵẖalqī).
adj. Innate; inborn. See اصلي
